mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when unemployment rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, creating a favorable earnings environment that drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales growth exceeds 2% annually while CPI inflation remains below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which improves corporate profit margins and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases of more than 1% month-over-month fail to precede positive 5-day forward returns in > 70% of observed cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5% forces markets to anticipate aggressive central bank rate hikes, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of months where CPI exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll increases of more than 150,000 per month fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a rank IC above 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts alongside robust real GDP growth signal strong aggregate demand and construction sector activity, which boosts capital goods orders and equity valuations, yielding positive forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of housing starts above 1.2 million and GDP growth above 2% annually fails to generate positive 20-day returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 98.0 index level indicates severe manufacturing contraction and supply-side weakness, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances when industrial production drops below 98.0.
